sections.heading: System Requirements
rows: Linux not supported — PyQt6 + TensorRT combo tested on Windows only
CUDA compute capability 6.0 minimum. RTX 30/40 series strongly recommended for TensorRT
YOLO11x-pose loads ~600 MB VRAM; 16 GB ensures headroom for stereo frames
Models: ~300 MB each, TensorRT engines: ~600 MB, calibration data: ~50 MB
Left camera + Right camera + ESP32 serial — all must be distinct physical ports
Not required when running compiled NEXUS.exe
Recommended 1920×1080 for sidebar + video feed to fit comfortably

sections.heading: Step 1 — NVIDIA Driver + CUDA
sections.content: Install the full NVIDIA toolchain in order. Each package depends on the previous one.
steps: NVIDIA Driver (latest) — Download from nvidia.com/drivers. Reboot after install.
CUDA Toolkit 11.8 or 12.x — Download from NVIDIA CUDA Downloads. Run the installer, keep default paths.
cuDNN 8.x or 9.x — Download from NVIDIA cuDNN (requires free NVIDIA Developer account). Extract and copy files to your CUDA installation directory.
TensorRT 8.x or 10.x — Download from NVIDIA TensorRT. Extract and add the lib/ folder to your system PATH.

sections.heading: Step 2 — ESP32 USB Driver
sections.content: The ESP32 DevKit uses a CH340 USB-to-Serial chip. Windows may not include this driver by default.
steps: Download the CH340 driver from WCH CH341SER
Run SETUP.EXE from the extracted zip and click INSTALL
Plug in your ESP32 and open Device Manager → Ports (COM & LPT) — you should see USB-SERIAL CH340 (COMx)
Note the COM number (e.g., COM3, COM8) — you will need this in Settings

sections.heading: Step 3 — Visual C++ Redistributable
sections.content: Required for PyQt6 and OpenCV DLLs to load correctly on Windows.
steps: Run the installer for both x64 and x86 versions

sections.heading: Step 4 — Python Packages (Source Install Only)
sections.content: If running from source rather than the compiled .exe, install all Python dependencies:
sections.code:
# Core UI and Vision
pip install PyQt6 opencv-python numpy

# AI + GPU Inference
pip install ultralytics torch torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118

# Serial Communication
pip install pyserial

# Math and calibration
pip install scipy

# Optional: MATLAB bridge
pip install matlabengine  # Must match your installed MATLAB version exactly

sections.heading: Step 5 — Build TensorRT Engine
sections.content: TensorRT engines are GPU-specific and must be built on the machine that will run NEXUS. This is a one-time process per GPU per model. Engines are typically 5–10× faster than raw .pt inference.
sections.code:
from ultralytics import YOLO
import os

# Choose your model (trade accuracy for speed)
models = [
    'models/yolo11x-pose.pt',   # Highest accuracy, ~15 FPS
    'models/yolo11m-pose.pt',   # Balanced,         ~25 FPS
    'models/yolo11s-pose.pt',   # Fast,              ~40 FPS
    'models/yolo11n-pose.pt',   # Fastest,           ~60 FPS
]

for m in models:
    if os.path.exists(m):
        print(f"Building engine for {m}...")
        model = YOLO(m)
        model.export(format='engine', device=0, half=True)  # half=True for FP16
        print(f"Done: {m.replace('.pt', '.engine')}")

sections.heading: Step 6 — Flash ESP32 Firmware
sections.content: The ESP32 must run the NEXUS firmware before hardware control will work.
steps: Install ESP32 board support: File → Preferences → add https://raw.githubusercontent.com/espressif/arduino-esp32/gh-pages/package_esp32_index.json to Board Manager URLs
Install the board: Tools → Board Manager → search ESP32 → install esp32 by Espressif Systems
Select Board: ESP32 Dev Module and correct COM port
Click Upload. After flashing, open Serial Monitor @ 115200 baud to verify the heartbeat is streaming.

sections.heading: Project Directory Structure
sections.code:
NEXUS/
├── main.py                    # Application entry point
├── config.py                  # Global CONFIG dictionary + load/save
├── settings.json              # Auto-generated user settings
├── models/
│   ├── yolo11x-pose.pt        # Downloaded YOLO weights
│   └── yolo11x-pose.engine    # TensorRT engine (built locally)
├── data/
│   ├── logo.png               # Dashboard logo
│   ├── stereo_calibration_py.npz   # Python calibration output
│   ├── stereo_calibration_bridge.npz  # MATLAB bridge output
│   └── laser_alignment.npz    # Laser homography matrix
├── core/
│   ├── vision_engine.py       # VisionEngine QThread — main AI loop
│   ├── predictor.py           # GhostPredictor Kalman smoother
│   ├── hardware_link.py       # TacticalSerial ESP32 comms
│   ├── math_calib.py          # CalibrationMath pure functions
│   └── python_calib_worker.py # Stereo calibration background thread
├── gui/
│   ├── components/
│   │   └── video_widget.py    # TacticalVideoWidget (click-enabled)
│   ├── tab_dashboard.py
│   ├── tab_hardware.py
│   ├── tab_laser.py
│   ├── tab_pycalib.py
│   ├── tab_stereo.py
│   ├── tab_matlab.py
│   ├── tab_bridge.py
│   └── tab_settings.py
└── firmware/
    └── nexus_guardian_v2.2.ino  # ESP32 Arduino firmware

sections.heading: First Launch Notes
sections.alertContent: On first launch: NEXUS auto-generates settings.json with safe default values. You must configure CAM_L_IDX, CAM_R_IDX, and SERIAL_PORT in the Settings tab before starting the engine. The engine will fail to start if cameras cannot be opened at the specified indices.
